meta.note: Public reading-copy index. Each text is emitted separately under public/source-texts so source pages load only the requested work.

sources.author: Rene Descartes
sources.title: Discourse on the Method
sources.relevance: Part V: an automaton could not arrange speech or action to meet the varied occurrences of life, because reason is a universal instrument.

sources.author: Thomas Hobbes
sources.title: Leviathan
sources.relevance: Reason defined as reckoning (ch. V): the one early-modern position on which a perfect reckoner reasons. Foundational for the reckoning-question grounds list.

sources.author: Mary Shelley
sources.title: Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus (1818 edition)
sources.relevance: Maker responsibility and the creature's demand for companionship; a labeled pre-1860 anchor.

sources.author: Edgar Allan Poe
sources.title: Maelzel's Chess-Player
sources.relevance: Calculation is fixed and determinate; chess depends on the variable judgment of the players. Grounds the choice-is-not-calculation position.

sources.author: Ada Lovelace
sources.title: Notes to the Sketch of the Analytical Engine
sources.relevance: Note G: the engine has no pretensions to originate anything. The founding denial in the reckoning benchmark.

sources.author: Henry James Sr.
sources.title: Lectures and Miscellanies
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q77; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: John Stuart Mill
sources.title: Considerations on Representative Government
sources.relevance: Fitness for self-government, participation, political education, and the franchise.

sources.author: Francisque Bouillier
sources.title: Du principe vital et de l'ame pensante
sources.relevance: The vitalist case that mind requires a principle no arrangement of matter supplies.

sources.author: Charles Babbage
sources.title: Passages from the Life of a Philosopher
sources.relevance: Teaching mechanism to foresee and to act upon that foresight: mental words applied to machinery.

sources.author: Henry James Sr.
sources.title: Substance and Shadow
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q77, Q78; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Karl Marx
sources.title: Capital, Volume I, Chapter 15: Machinery and Modern Industry
sources.relevance: Machinery, factory discipline, displacement, and the worker's relation to the production process.

sources.author: John Stuart Mill
sources.title: The Subjection of Women
sources.relevance: Legal subordination, government of one's own person, and women's political capacity.

sources.author: Charles Darwin
sources.title: The Descent of Man, and Selection in Relation to Sex
sources.relevance: Continuity of human and animal mental faculties and the evolution of moral and intellectual powers.

sources.author: Samuel Butler
sources.title: Erewhon
sources.relevance: Machine evolution, dependence, succession, and destruction of machines.

sources.author: T. H. Huxley
sources.title: On the Hypothesis That Animals Are Automata, and Its History
sources.relevance: Original formulation of conscious automatism and its physiological evidence.

sources.author: William Stanley Jevons
sources.title: The Principles of Science, vol. I
sources.relevance: The logical piano: the true process of logical inference accomplished in a purely mechanical manner, with conscious thought at the edges. First edition; the bench cites the 1877 second edition wording, present here.

sources.author: Ernst Kapp
sources.title: Grundlinien einer Philosophie der Technik
sources.relevance: Technology as organ projection: machines understood through the human body rather than as an autonomous external species.

sources.author: Henry George
sources.title: Progress and Poverty
sources.relevance: Poverty amid technological progress and the social production and appropriation of wealth.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: Are We Automata?
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q57; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Henry James Sr.
sources.title: Society the Redeemed Form of Man
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q17, Q77, Q78; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Eduardo Ladislao Holmberg
sources.title: Horacio Kalibang o los automatas
sources.relevance: Artificial persons and automata used to stage a materialist-spiritualist dispute in late nineteenth-century Argentina.

sources.author: John Venn
sources.title: Symbolic Logic
sources.relevance: Logical machines can perform only a small part of the process of reasoning; framing and interpretation demand skill and sagacity (pp. 119-21).

sources.author: Didier de Chousy
sources.title: Ignis
sources.relevance: Automated industry, manufactured workers, capitalist control, labor conflict, and technological catastrophe.

sources.author: William Morris
sources.title: Useful Work versus Useless Toil
sources.relevance: Meaningful work, toil, machinery, and the social conditions of liberation from labor.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: Introduction to The Literary Remains of the Late Henry James
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q77; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: The Principles of Psychology, Volume 1
sources.relevance: Habit, automaton theory, brain function, and association.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: The Principles of Psychology, Volume 2
sources.relevance: Emotion, competing desires, effort of attention, and will.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: The Principles of Psychology
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q1, Q8, Q70; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: Psychology (Briefer Course)
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q8, Q57, Q70; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Alfred Binet
sources.title: Psychologie des grands calculateurs et joueurs d'echecs
sources.relevance: Psychological evidence that chess play requires selective attention and judgment of relevance, unlike procedure-bound calculation.

sources.author: Gustave Le Bon
sources.title: The Crowd: A Study of the Popular Mind
sources.relevance: Individual judgment and agency in a psychological crowd.

sources.author: H. G. Wells
sources.title: The Time Machine
sources.relevance: Narrative vision of cosmic decline and the dying Earth.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: The Will to Believe and Other Essays in Popular Philosophy
sources.relevance: Includes The Dilemma of Determinism, The Will to Believe, and Great Men and Their Environment.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: The Will to Believe and Other Essays
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q57; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Paolo Mantegazza
sources.title: L'anno 3000
sources.relevance: Artificial vital power, planetary infrastructure, medical governance, thought-reading, and the gap between technical and moral progress.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: Talks to Teachers on Psychology
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q17, Q34; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Victor Anestin
sources.title: In anul 4000 sau o calatorie la Venus
sources.relevance: A Romanian technological future and voyage to Venus, useful for testing non-Anglophone ideas of progress and planetary civilization.

sources.author: Jagadish Chandra Bose
sources.title: Response in the Living and Non-Living
sources.relevance: Experimental comparison of electrical response in metals, plants, and animal tissue that disputes a hard boundary between living and non-living matter.

sources.author: Bertrand Russell
sources.title: A Free Man's Worship, in Modern Essays
sources.relevance: Human value and action in an indifferent, ultimately lifeless universe.

sources.author: W. E. B. Du Bois
sources.title: The Souls of Black Folk
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q17; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain
sources.title: Sultana's Dream
sources.relevance: A feminist technological utopia in which solar power, scientific education, and altered gender relations are inseparable.

sources.author: Valery Bryusov
sources.title: The Republic of the Southern Cross
sources.relevance: A highly administered industrial republic whose apparent order collapses through collective behavioral breakdown.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: Pragmatism
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q1; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: H. G. Wells
sources.title: The War in the Air
sources.relevance: Technological power turning cities and civilization into targets.

sources.author: Francis Galton
sources.title: Memories of My Life
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q1; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Wu Jianren
sources.title: Xin Shitou Ji (New Story of the Stone)
sources.relevance: A late-Qing technological future used to ask how scientific capacity, political order, and national self-determination fit together.

sources.author: Horacio Quiroga (published as S. Fragoso Lima)
sources.title: El hombre artificial
sources.relevance: A Buenos Aires magazine serial about manufacturing a human capable of sensation, thought, and suffering; obscure within a famous author's oeuvre.

sources.author: Frederick Winslow Taylor
sources.title: The Principles of Scientific Management
sources.relevance: Task decomposition, managerial control over method and pace, and worker discretion.

sources.author: Jerzy Zulawski
sources.title: Stara Ziemia (The Old Earth)
sources.relevance: Technological modernity, social control, automation, and estrangement in the final volume of the Lunar Trilogy.

sources.author: Walther Rathenau
sources.title: Zur Mechanik des Geistes
sources.relevance: A critique of social and intellectual mechanization that locates the danger in institutions and habits rather than in autonomous machines.

sources.author: Miguel de Unamuno
sources.title: Mecanopolis
sources.relevance: A city populated and governed only by machines, presented as both alluring and intolerably inhuman.

sources.author: J. S. Haldane
sources.title: Mechanism, Life and Personality
sources.relevance: Lecture I states the organism's apparent autonomy of its own ends, then criticizes the vitalist reading of it. 1921 printing of the 1913 lectures.

sources.author: H. G. Wells
sources.title: The World Set Free
sources.relevance: Civilization-scale technological danger and atomic war.

sources.author: Frigyes Karinthy
sources.title: Utazas Faremidoba
sources.relevance: A society of intelligent machine beings that regards organic humanity and war as diseased forms of life.

sources.author: Alexei Gastev
sources.title: Poeziia rabochego udara
sources.relevance: Worker poetry representing the factory, the machine, and the laboring body as mutually transforming systems.

sources.author: William James
sources.title: Collected Essays and Reviews
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q1; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: W. E. B. Du Bois
sources.title: Darkwater: Voices from Within the Veil
sources.relevance: Semantic-search discovery in Jamesiana for questions Q17, Q34, Q78; human gold tagging pending.

sources.author: Sigmund Freud
sources.title: Group Psychology and the Analysis of the Ego
sources.relevance: Identification, suggestion, and altered individual agency in groups.

sources.author: Walter Lippmann
sources.title: Public Opinion
sources.relevance: Pseudo-environments, stereotypes, news, and the formation of public opinion.

sources.author: Rodolfo Teofilo
sources.title: O Reino de Kiato
sources.relevance: A Brazilian planned utopia connecting public health, eugenics, education, governance, and personal freedom.

sources.author: Karel Capek
sources.title: R.U.R. (Rossum's Universal Robots)
sources.relevance: Artificial workers, exploitation, personhood, and revolt.

sources.author: Wakizo Hosoi
sources.title: Sagyo Kikai (Work Machine)
sources.relevance: A proletarian poem in which the machine does not liberate the operator: the worker becomes part of its mechanism and is physically consumed by it.

sources.author: Yevgeny Zamyatin
sources.title: We
sources.relevance: Numbered citizens, scheduled life, regulated intimacy, and dangerous imagination.

sources.author: Karel Capek
sources.title: Injo Nodongja (Artificial Laborer)
sources.relevance: A Korean translation of R.U.R., serialized in Kaebyok from February through May 1925; valuable for tracing colonial reception, but not an independent historical source for the idea.

sources.author: Edward Bernays
sources.title: Propaganda
sources.relevance: Deliberate organization of public habits and opinions.

sources.author: E. M. Forster
sources.title: The Eternal Moment and Other Stories
sources.relevance: Includes The Machine Stops: infrastructural dependence, isolation, machine worship, and collapse.

sources.author: Riichi Yokomitsu
sources.title: Kikai (Machine)
sources.relevance: Factory work and suspicion rendered through an unseen social machine that measures people and drives their actions; included near the end of the 1850–1940 period.
